title: jungia pauciflora subsp. amplistipula
intro: Jungia pauciflora subsp. amplistipula is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of South America. It is also known as the "Lupuna" tree due to its distinctive trunk. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Jungia pauciflora subsp. amplistipula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing.
